name: Fathom
agent_said: developers.fathom.ai, API key self-serve in minutes
docs_say: Confirmed: developers.fathom.ai is the fathom.video meeting product; 'Generate an API key and make your first call in minutes'

name: Fathom
docs_say: That repo is for Fathom Analytics (website stats) — a different company than the fathom.video meeting recorder

name: Mermaid CLI
docs_say: No auth at all — mermaid-cli is a local npm CLI (mmdc), no hosted API, no credentials. Agent bled in API-key text from unrelated search results

name: WhatsApp Business
docs_say: Bearer access tokens (temporary token for testing, permanent system-user token in production)

name: WhatsApp Business
docs_say: Confirmed: free test number + temporary token, no payment or approval to start

name: Fanbasis
docs_say: Confirmed: apidocs.fan is FanBasis; x-api-key header, key copied from dashboard, sandbox available

name: PitchBook
docs_say: 'The API is a separate offering from the PitchBook platform that requires a standalone contract agreement' — gated behind sales, not buildable self-serve

name: Waterfall.io
agent_said: partial — master API key on signup, likely paid
docs_say: Right entity (contact/company enrichment); docs confirm x-api-key and are silent on pricing — the hedge is the honest answer

name: Ahrefs
agent_said: partial — API key requires paid Ahrefs plan
docs_say: Confirmed: API keys created in account settings on paid plans; no free API tier

name: Telegram
docs_say: Confirmed: bot token from @BotFather; api_id/api_hash for MTProto apps

name: Shopify
agent_said: OAuth2 + session tokens, self-serve via custom app in dev dashboard
docs_say: Confirmed: custom apps installable on a dev store generate access tokens without review; OAuth for public apps

name: Amazon Selling Partner
agent_said: partial — requires Amazon approval for data access
docs_say: Confirmed: developer registration and role-based approval required before SP-API access

name: Zendesk
docs_say: Free 14-day trial has full API access, and Zendesk sponsors non-expiring dev instances — self-serve for developers
